sentences.text: Hey there, Jessie.
Can you hear me?
All right.
Hello?
Sorry.

There we go.
That's it.
How are you?
How are you today?
Ditto uh Good.
I mean, Jinx, I'm good.
I'm good.
How about yourself?
Good.
It's been busy but um all is good so far today.
Good.
Love to hear that.
I know it's uh for us, at least.

Um You guys have been with us a little while so site to, to get some feedback here.

Um wanted to highlight uh some changes that we made in the market survey report just to make sure you're aware of them.
Um You have access to them.
So I wanna make sure your teams are using them.
Uh Talk a little bit about the usage metrics.
I know you wanted some reporting.
So I have some good news for you there and I've got some stuff to share with you.
So just kind of aligning on what you'd like to see on a weekly basis.
Um And then maybe talking a little bit more about the success metrics as a whole.
Uh So that when we do that quarterly business review next quarter, uh we've got some nice kind of uh metrics to align on and make sure that we're hitting the targets.
We wanted anything you wanna add to that?
No, that sounds all great.
Cool.
Um So then let's start first with some feedback.
Uh How, how are your teams finding things, anything bubbling up on your end?
Is it mostly positive?
Uh Any issues?
I actually talked with two managers this morning and they both uh have been using it.
Um And I just wanna make sure everyone's tried it so far.
That's why the, you know, seeing the usage and log in is important to me.
But a few of my, you know, more senior managers um have reached out saying that they love it.
Um So everything's good on that end.
There was one thing I saw this morning and now I can't remember which property it was for.
Um But it wasn't pulling and I put a suggest update on it.
Um,
but it was one of them wasn't like I was looking at the pricing availability, you know, on the market survey fee and taking it to minimum and then grouped at me at metric.
Um, but it wasn't showing, it said like, for example, our one bedroom was at 1410.
Um, when in reality, it's 1305 is our actual lowest.
So it was just one.
But um I, I, I'm actually going through market surveys today.
Um the ones that the team puts together um and kind of doing some adjustments on um loss leaders right now.
Um So that's why I'm looking at it.
OK.
No, that's great.
That's great feedback.
I'm sure.

You just take a look at that, look at the suggest updates that came in and just make sure we get that resolved.
Probably a little bit of a priori prioritization issue.
Um So those are pretty quick and easy to resolve.
Awesome.
Glad to hear that most people are liking it though.
The two senior managers getting bought in.
I think that's great.
Uh I think I know at least in our experience it can be tough rolling out a new software, right?
Rolling out a new tool.
So great to hear that, that senior folks, uh, you know, I feel like they kind of set the tone for the rest of the team when you have your senior managers buy in.
It's a great, it's a great thing.
Yeah.
And it actually makes their life easier so that everyone should be buying it.

So, I just want to make sure people are, like, using it because it's like, you know, sometimes people are so set in their ways and they're like, oh, there's too much to learn, but it's like this is, will save you hours.
So, I agree.
Yeah, I know.
It's always so frustrating to be on my end of the stick.
And I know the kind of problems that, that a lot of organizations are having people telling me, hey, it just takes too much time to do XYZ.
Well, then log into IQ because it's gonna save you a time.
So I know that sometimes that could be a tough message.

Uh, glad that's happening.

right?
